title: Parkio Daily for June 7, 2026
teaser: Polynesian bus depot opens; Mickey and Minnie debut Halloween costumes; donut flights land in Disney Springs.

rides.name: Soarin'
rides.note: Posted 35-minute wait at 9am, typically low before 11am

rides.name: Big Thunder Mountain
rides.note: Recently reopened, drawing moderate crowds mid-morning

rides.name: Muppet*Vision 3D
rides.note: Wait times climbing as nostalgia drives repeat visits

bignews.title: Polynesian Village bus depot now complete
bignews.body: The new bus station beneath the monorail beam at Disney's Polynesian Village Resort finished construction this week. Walls are down and furniture is in place, though the depot has not yet opened for guest use.

parkioInsight.text: Once operational, this depot may offer faster loading than the old stops during morning park opening windows. Monitor resort signage for the official start date.

bignews.title: Mickey and Minnie get Halloween costumes for Disneyland
bignews.body: Disneyland shared preview images of new Halloween outfits for Mickey and Minnie Mouse debuting during the fall season this year. The makeover arrives as part of the resort's 2026 Halloween celebration plans.

parkioInsight.text: Character meet queues tend to spike in the first week after costume debuts. Visiting during the second or third week of the season usually offers shorter waits for photos.

bignews.title: Donut flights debut at Disney Springs
bignews.body: A new mocktail donut flight platter is now available at Amorette's Patisserie in Disney Springs. Early reviews praise the pairing concept, though some flavors landed better than others.

parkioInsight.text: Amorette's display cases tend to run low on signature items after 3pm. Stopping by mid-morning or early afternoon often ensures the full flight selection is available.

bignews.title: Rare character costumes appear at DVC events
bignews.body: Disney Vacation Club member gatherings have introduced seldom-seen character outfits not regularly available in the parks. The appearances mark a shift in how DVC activates member perks.

parkioInsight.text: DVC events are typically smaller and less crowded than in-park meet-and-greets. Members attending may find shorter photo lines and longer interaction windows.

topstories.title: Stitch merchandise released for 626 Day
topstories.body: Two new Lilo and Stitch items dropped at Disneyland this week in time for June 26 (6/26), the annual fan holiday celebrating Experiment 626. A Laundry Hero plush and companion piece are both in stock.

parkioInsight.text: Limited-edition Stitch drops often sell out within 48 hours at Disneyland stores. Checking World of Disney or Emporium early in the day may secure inventory before it moves online-only.

topstories.title: Country Bears spotted at EPCOT for character appearances
topstories.body: Guests reported seeing Country Bear characters greeting in EPCOT this week, an unusual placement for the typically Magic Kingdom-based group. The appearances have not been officially announced.

parkioInsight.text: Unscheduled character appearances tend to draw smaller crowds than advertised meets. If you spot the Bears in World Showcase, photo queues may be shorter than usual.

topstories.title: Pastel Walt Disney World apparel arrives at resorts
topstories.body: Yellow and pink pastel sweatshirts and tank tops bearing the Walt Disney World logo are now in stock at Riverside Depot in Animal Kingdom and Keystone Clothiers in Hollywood Studios.

parkioInsight.text: New apparel designs often vanish from shelves within a week. Grabbing the item on your first park day typically avoids a sold-out situation later in your trip.

topstories.title: Earl of Sandwich construction shows interior lighting in Downtown Disney
topstories.body: Patio furniture and interior fixtures are now visible inside the upcoming Earl of Sandwich and Gordon Ramsay Carnaby location at Downtown Disney. Exterior facade work is nearly complete.

parkioInsight.text: New dining locations often soft-open without much notice. Checking the Downtown Disney directory board daily may catch an early preview window before the official opening rush.

topstories.title: Star Wars racing apparel and Mandalorian gear arrive at Disneyland
topstories.body: A collection of Star Wars racing-themed sweatshirts and a Mandalorian gauntlet-and-shield set are now on shelves at Disneyland Resort, matching items previously released online and at Walt Disney World.

parkioInsight.text: Star Wars merchandise tends to move fastest at Galaxy's Edge shops. Stopping by Dok-Ondar's before noon usually ensures the widest selection before popular sizes sell out.

topstories.title: Tomorrowland quietly continues aesthetic refresh at Magic Kingdom
topstories.body: Imagineering has been steadily updating Tomorrowland signage, color schemes, and landscaping over the past year to move away from dated design elements. The changes are ongoing but not widely advertised.

parkioInsight.text: Construction scaffolding and paint crews tend to be most visible in Tomorrowland mornings before 11am. Scheduling your visit to this land after lunch often avoids the bulk of the work zones.

topstories.title: Japan Pavilion bread selection draws positive reviews at EPCOT
topstories.body: A recently expanded bread counter at the Japan Pavilion in EPCOT World Showcase has earned strong guest feedback for quality and variety, adding a new quick-service option to the pavilion.

parkioInsight.text: Freshly baked items in World Showcase often sell out by mid-afternoon. Grabbing bread at the Japan Pavilion during your morning loop usually ensures the best selection.

icymi.title: Monstropolis construction continues at Hollywood Studios
icymi.body: Steel framework is rising behind walls at the new Monsters Inc. land under construction at Disney's Hollywood Studios. The Disney Parks Blog shared progress photos showing vertical work on multiple structures.

icymi.title: Alaska Airlines unveils Toy Story 5 themed plane
icymi.body: Alaska Airlines partnered with Disney to wrap one aircraft in Toy Story 5 livery ahead of the film's release. The plane is now in service on select routes.

icymi.title: Disneyland Halloween details announced for fall 2026
icymi.body: The Disney Parks Blog published a full rundown of Halloween and fall programming coming to Disneyland Resort this year, including ride overlays, seasonal merchandise, and themed food items.

icymi.title: MagicBands continue slow decline at Disneyland and Walt Disney World
icymi.body: Usage and availability of MagicBand wearables have dropped steadily over the past few years, with Disneyland seeing particularly low adoption and Walt Disney World scaling back inventory.

spotlight.title: Bluey's Wild World opens at Animal Kingdom this month
spotlight.body: Bluey, Bingo, and Australian wildlife arrive at Disney's Animal Kingdom on May 26 as part of the Cool Kids Summer programming. The experience includes character meet-and-greets and themed activities that will run beyond the summer season.

parkioInsight.text: New character experiences tend to draw families with young children, especially during morning hours. Animal Kingdom's Discovery Island may see heavier foot traffic between 10am and noon while Bluey meet times are active.
